codeReview.summary: The feature adds broad authorization coverage for org/scenario/employee endpoints and extensive tests, but one blocking correctness gap remains in the scenario diff endpoint for non-existent IDs.

issues.description: diffScenarios intentionally proceeds when one or both scenario IDs are valid-but-nonexistent (comment: "return empty diff"), then queries employees and can return HTTP 200 with empty/partial diff data. This violates VAL-AUTHZ-007, which requires non-existent org/scenario IDs to return 403 or 404 and explicitly forbids 200-with-empty-data behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation-worker skill mandates strict tests-first TDD for backend changes, but this feature's transcript shows implementation work (creating authorization middleware/routes/controllers) occurred before creating authorization tests. This appears to be a practical mismatch for authz middleware work rather than negligence, so skill guidance may need refinement.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md:28-33 requires tests before implementation; worker transcript skeleton (worker-transcripts.jsonl line 6) shows "First, let me create the authorization middleware helper" and later "Now let me write comprehensive tests."

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture documentation is stale after this feature: it still says org memberIds are not used in endpoints, but authorization now depends on memberIds/owner checks across scenario and employee access paths.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:53 says "Organization.memberIds[] ... (org membership, not yet used in endpoints)", while commit 04a8903 adds requireOrgMembership/requireScenarioAccess and checkOrgMembership usage.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit 04a8903 diff, transcript skeleton, and skill docs. Result is FAIL due to a blocking gap: diffScenarios can return 200 for valid-but-nonexistent scenario IDs, conflicting with VAL-AUTHZ-007. Also noted shared-state gaps in skill procedure realism and stale architecture knowledge.
